summary.intent: Implement task #215: a new framework-agnostic ToggleButton core component in ars-components, combining Button's focus/press behavior with Toggle's controlled/uncontrolled Bindable<bool> pattern, with full TDD, snapshot tests, mutation testing, spec sync, and a GitHub PR.
summary.outcome: The ToggleButton core was fully implemented and all required verification gates passed: 23 unit tests, 9 snapshots, 0 mutation survivors (82 mutants, 61 caught, 21 unviable), 94.63% line coverage, clippy clean, and all 10 cargo xci fast gates passed. A commit was made and a PR push was initiated.

repo: Components follow a strict TDD + post-implementation-audit workflow: failing tests first, then implementation, then two consecutive clean audit rounds before mutation testing.
cargo xtask provides spec tooling: `cargo xtask spec digest <component>` and `cargo xtask spec validate` are required reading and validation steps before/after editing spec markdown.

cargo xci --profile fast is the pre-push gate; it runs 10 stages (fmt, check, clippy, unit nextest ~5943 tests, i18n split units, integration, adapter, spec snippets, error-variant coverage, mutual-exclusion/snapshot-count).
New agnostic components require a targeted `cargo mutants -p ars-components -f <file>` run with zero untriaged survivors before the task is considered complete.
The GitHub Project board item must be moved to 'In Progress' at the start of implementation using `gh project item-edit` with project-specific IDs.
Adapter specs (Leptos, Dioxus) and the core spec must be kept in sync; the post-implementation audit explicitly checks adapter-spec drift in two consecutive clean passes.
Bindable<bool> is the standard pattern for controlled/uncontrolled boolean state across utility components (Toggle, ToggleButton).
Hidden input rendering is adapter-owned; the core component returns a pure HiddenInputConfig struct, not a HiddenInput anatomy part.
The `cargo insta test --unreferenced=reject` flag can fail on pre-existing unreferenced snapshots outside the current task scope, so the scoped form `-- toggle_button` is the correct workaround.

code.finding: New module created. Uses Bindable<bool> for pressed state, PendingEffect<Machine> with no_cleanup() for PressedChange, and mirrors structural conventions from utility/button/mod.rs and utility/toggle/mod.rs.
Added `pub mod toggle_button;` export. This is the required registration point for new utility components.
Spec conformance anatomy test added: asserts scope 'toggle-button' maps to Part::Root -> 'root'. This is the required pattern for all utility components.

workflow: cargo mutants buffers output heavily; survivors may only appear near the end of a run, so early silence does not indicate a clean pass.
Multiple mutation runs were needed (3 total): first pass revealed 11 survivors, second pass revealed 2, third pass was clean. Each survivor required a targeted test addition, not a code change.

The `cargo insta test` CLI syntax in plan documentation (`cargo insta test -p ars-components toggle_button --unreferenced=reject`) did not match the installed version; the correct form is `cargo insta test -p ars-components --unreferenced=reject -- toggle_button`.
Removing a `const` qualifier from a function that calls non-const methods (like Bindable::get()) is a common early compiler error when porting patterns between modules.
Post-implementation audit spec syncs (adapter specs, core spec) should be landed within the same PR as the implementation, not deferred.

friction: `cargo insta test -p ars-components --unreferenced=reject` failed on pre-existing unreferenced snapshots in utility/highlight and other unrelated components, blocking the full snapshot gate without any way to scope it to just the new component.
Plan documentation contained an incorrect `cargo insta` CLI invocation that didn't match the installed version, requiring discovery of the correct flag order mid-session.
Initial implementation hit a `const fn` restriction: a helper marked `const` called `Bindable::get()` which is non-const, causing a compiler error.
A test naming collision (local `service` variable shadowing a helper) caused a test suite failure after adding new mutation-triage tests, requiring a second fix pass.
`cargo mutants` produced no progress output during runs, making it impossible to distinguish a slow-but-running pass from a hang until the final summary appeared.
Three full mutation runs were required to reach 0 survivors, each taking significant wall time due to workspace rebuild overhead.
open_items: Pre-existing unreferenced snapshots in utility/highlight and other components block `cargo insta test --unreferenced=reject` at the workspace level; these need a separate cleanup task.
Existing rustdoc broken-link warnings across unrelated ars-components modules were not addressed in this task.
Leptos and Dioxus adapter implementations for ToggleButton are explicitly out of scope for #215 and will require separate issues.
PR creation and the post-push Codex review loop (waiting-for-codex-review skill) were initiated at the end of the transcript but not confirmed as completed within the session.
